entry.title: SAP Integration Suite : GitHub integration in SAP Cloud Integration
entry.content:
Introduction 

SAP Cloud Integration version 6.80/(8.x --> 8.44 ), one of the capabilities of SAP Integration Suite, comes with a feature which will allow you to integrate the GitHub to the integration suite to perform the basic GitHub operations on the design time integration artifacts. To know more, you can refer the help documentation.

In this blog post, you will understand the detailed aspects of this feature, Why, What, its objectives, principles and how to leverage in the Cloud integration capability of the integration suite.

Why GitHub integration is essential?

  • No GitHub integration in the Integration Suite for collaborative development, track changes, storage, backup, or recovery.
  • Users must manually download and upload artifacts in GitHub, causing media breakage.
  • Manual process is slow, error-prone, and impacts version control, reducing productivity for effective collaborative developments.

What is GitHub Integration?

  • Integrates Integration Suite with GitHub for secure repository connection, Git authentication, and operations directly in-app.
  • Streamlines artifact management, reduces errors, enhances version control, and supports collaborative developments and CI/CD compatibility.

Benefits/Value proposition

  • Collaboration: Streamlined teamwork via branches and pull requests
  • Seamless Integration : No media breakage between Integration suite and GitHub
  • Governance : Github repositories maintained in Integration suite via Tenant administrator
  • DevOps: Enables CI/CD automation
  • Backup: Secure storage for artifacts with easy recovery.
  • Distributed: Work locally and sync changes.
  • Rollback: Revert to previous versions quickly.
  • Traceability: Track changes for auditing.
  • Scalability: Supports large and distributed teams.
  • Security: Protect artifacts with permissions and authentication.

Availability

GitHub integration in the integration suite for cloud integration capability is available in the trial, standard and above/higher editions.

How to leverage?

Let me explain the workflow and steps into two parts.

  • How to Successfully Configure GitHub Repositories for Cloud Integration?
  • How to successfully import/clone, push, Pull the integration artifacts from/to Github?

How to Successfully Configure GitHub Repositories for Cloud Integration?

To integrate GitHub repositories successfully, follow these easy steps:

Prerequisites:

  1. Ensure your account has a Tenant Admin role (refer to Cloud Integration documentation if unsure).
  2. Generate a Personal Access Token (PAT) with the required permissions for the repositories.

How to Create a Personal Access Token (PAT):

  1. Log in to GitHub, go to Settings -- >  Developer Settings -- > Personal Access Tokens.
  2. Choose Fine-Grained or Classic Tokens and click Generate New Token. Set an expiry for the token.

Simple Setup Steps:

  1. In the Cloud Integration portal, navigate to Settings -- > Integrations -- > Git Access.
  2. Click Edit in the Repository Details table and select Add to set up a new repository.
  3. Enter your PAT and GitHub repository URL.
  4. Click Connect to link the repository (Name and Description will auto-fill).
  5. Click Add to save the repository details in the table.

Notes:

  • You can integrate up to 50 repositories.
  • Make sure to click Save after completing all configurations.

Follow these steps to configure your GitHub repositories for seamless Cloud Integration!

View Github Repostories

Add Github Repository

Edit Github Repository 

How to successfully import/clone, push, Pull the integration artifacts from/to Github?

  • Git Import use case & experience 
  • Git Push use case & experience
  • Git Pull use case and experience

Git Import use case & experience 

Prerequisites:

  • Ensure Git repositories are configured by the tenant admin.
  • Have a Personal Access Token (PAT) with permission for the repositories.

Steps for Importing Integration Flows:

  1. In the Design section, go to Integration Packages.
  2. Select an integration package and click Edit.
  3. Open the Artifacts tab and select Add Integration Flow.
  4. Choose Add Integrations Manually (requires Premium plan).
  5. Use Git Import to import the flow:
    • Enter your PAT.
    • Select a repository and branch.
    • Choose the desired flow and click Add or Add and Open in Editor.

Outcome:
The flow is imported into the integration package, enabling Git operations like push and pull for collaboration and version control

Add Integration flow from Github repository, branch

Git Push use case & experience

Git Push lets you upload local changes from an integration artifact to a connected remote Git repository, ensuring your repository is fully updated for collaboration and version control.

Prerequisites Before Git Push:

  • Configured Repository: Your admin should set up the necessary repositories (see Git Access guide for details).

  • Access Permissions: Have the right permissions and a Personal Access Token (PAT) ready.

Steps to Push Changes:

  1. Navigate to Integration Packages: Go to Design > Integrations and APIs to find the list of integration packages.

  2. Select the Package: Choose the package with the artifact you want to push and click Artifacts.

  3. Start Git Push: Click the ... (triple dot) icon on the artifact and select Git Push.

  4. Fill in the Details:

    • Add your PAT for authentication.

    • Choose the repository name and branch.

  5. Commit Message: Enter a clear message describing your changes.

  6. Push Changes: Click Push to upload updates to the remote repository.

Key Notes:

  • The repository will be updated with the latest version of your artifact.

  • Ensure branch and repository details are correct to avoid errors or conflicts.

Git Push operation to push the local changes to the Github repository, branch

Git Pull use case and experience

Git Pull updates your local files by fetching and merging the latest changes from a remote Git repository, keeping everything in sync.

Before You Start

  1. Ensure repositories are set up by the administrator (Check Git Access documentation).
  2. Git Pull works only for imported artifacts.
Steps to Perform Git Pull
  1. Open Integration Packages:
    Go to Design -- > Integrations and APIs to find integration packages.
  2. Select a Package:
    Click on the package and then choose Artifacts to view its contents.
  3. Use Git Pull:
    Find the artifact to update and click the Git Pull icon.
  4. Verify Details:
    Confirm the displayed artifact info and enter your Personal Access Token (PAT).
  5. Finish Up:
    Click Pull to update your local artifact with the latest changes.

This helps you easily keep your files updated for smooth collaboration.

Git Pull operation to pull the latest changes from the Github repository, branch

FAQs

  1. Supported Integration Artifacts: Basic Git operations are supported for Integration Flows in the initial release.
  2. Available Editions: GitHub integration is available in the Trial, Standard, and higher editions of the Integration Suite.
  3. Repository Limit: You can add up to 50 GitHub repositories in the integration settings.
  4. Impact of Removing Repositories: Removing repositories from the integration settings has no effect on integration flows—they remain in the design workspace and GitHub, if previously pushed. However, the integration flow will be de-linked from GitHub.
  5. Supported Platforms: Only GitHub on the cloud is supported on-premise GitHub is not.
  6. Supported Cloud Platform : GitLab.
  7. Authentication: GitHub operations are supported through PAT tokens only.
  8. PAT Token Persistence: PAT tokens are not store, you must manually provide one for each GitHub operation.
  9. Repository Descriptions: Descriptions for GitHub repositories can be set during creation on GitHub and will be visible when imported into the Integration Suite.
  10. Differentiate local and remote changes: Github repository name, branch name is tagged to the integration flow available in the remote branch. Integration flow is tagged in the design workspace and monitoring view(for deployed artifact).

What’s coming next?

  • Introduce CRUD functionality for user Personal Access Tokens (PAT).
  • Add support for more authentication types and cloud platforms like GitLab.
  • Extend compatibility with on-premise GitHub repositories.
  • Enhance Git operations like import, push, pull for the remaining integration artifacts.
  • Implement conflict resolution tools (graphical/text editor) for integration artifacts and resources.
  • Support multi-branch management for integration artifacts.
  • Enable public OData Remote APIs for CI/CD processes.
  • Expand Git support for integration packages.

Other source of material

  • https://help.sap.com/docs/integration-suite/isuite-integrations-and-apis/git-access
  • https://help.sap.com/docs/integration-suite/isuite-integrations-and-apis/git-push
  • https://help.sap.com/docs/integration-suite/isuite-integrations-and-apis/git-pull
  • https://help.sap.com/docs/integration-suite/isuite-integrations-and-apis/import-integration-flows-from-git-repository

I hope you find the feature in this blog post beneficial. If you have any questions or feedback, please leave a comment.

entry.title: Feedback on SAP Certification AI Scoring Transparency and Exam Reliability
entry.content:
I would like to share some constructive feedback regarding the SAP certification experience, particularly around the AI-based scoring process and the reliability of certification exams. I have attended the C_THR83 Performance and Goals Management where I had a very unreliable scoring and still awaiting response from SAP Learning Support Team!

While I appreciate SAP's continued investment in modernizing its certification program, I believe there is room for improvement in the transparency of the AI-based scoring system. At present, candidates receive only an overall score, with no visibility into how individual tasks or responses were evaluated. Without detailed feedback, it is difficult to understand where improvements are needed or to have confidence in the assessment outcome.

It would be beneficial if SAP could consider:

  • Providing a breakdown of scores by task or competency area.
  • Offering more detailed feedback on evaluated responses.
  • Introducing a formal score review or re-evaluation process for candidates who believe their results may warrant further review.

In addition to scoring transparency, many candidates have reported recurring technical issues during certification exams. Although SAP regularly performs maintenance on the certification platform, some issues continue to occur, including:

  • Portlets not displaying correctly.
  • Workflows not triggering despite Rule Trace confirming the business rules executed successfully.
  • Difficulties starting exams.
  • Other intermittent platform stability issues that can affect the overall exam experience.

Another area that could benefit from improvement is the responsiveness of support. When candidates encounter technical issues or have concerns about exam results, timely communication and clear updates from the support team are essential to maintaining confidence in the certification process.

SAP certifications represent a significant investment of time, effort, and resources for candidates. For that reason, transparency, technical reliability, and an effective support process are all important in ensuring that the certification experience is both fair and trusted.

I hope SAP will continue to enhance the certification program by:

  • Increasing transparency in AI-based scoring.
  • Providing candidates with more meaningful assessment feedback.
  • Introducing a structured review or appeal process for scoring.
  • Continuing to improve the stability and reliability of certification exams.
  • Strengthening support responsiveness and communication when candidates report issues.

These improvements would help reinforce confidence in SAP certifications and provide candidates with a clearer understanding of their performance while ensuring a more reliable and positive certification experience.

entry.title: SAP Incentive Management: Leveraging BusinessObjects APIs for Automated Reporting
entry.content:
Introduction

Incentive compensation plays a critical role in motivating sales teams and driving business performance. Organizations using SAP Incentive Management (formerly CallidusCloud Commissions) often rely on reports and analytics to monitor commissions, validate calculations, track performance, and provide timely insights to stakeholders.

While SAP Incentive Management offers powerful reporting capabilities through SAP BusinessObjects (BO), many organizations still depend on manual report generation and distribution processes. As reporting requirements grow, manual execution becomes time-consuming, error-prone, and difficult to scale.

This blog explores how SAP BusinessObjects APIs can be used to automate report scheduling, extraction, and distribution, enabling organizations to create efficient and scalable reporting solutions for SAP Incentive Management.

The Reporting Challenge

Typical incentive management reporting requirements include:

  • Commission statements for payees
  • Credit and payment summaries
  • Sales performance dashboards
  • Compensation plan effectiveness reports
  • Audit and compliance reports
  • Monthly and quarterly executive reporting

Organizations frequently encounter challenges such as:

  • Manual report execution
  • Repetitive report distribution activities
  • Delays in report availability
  • Dependency on administrators
  • Difficulty integrating reports with external systems

Automation addresses these challenges by allowing reports to be generated and delivered without human intervention.

Why Use BusinessObjects APIs?

SAP Incentive Management reporting is commonly built on the SAP BusinessObjects platform. BusinessObjects provides a comprehensive set of APIs through its RESTful Web Services that enable developers to programmatically interact with reports and report schedules.

Using BO APIs, organizations can:

  • Schedule reports automatically
  • Execute reports on demand
  • Retrieve report outputs in multiple formats
  • Monitor report execution status
  • Distribute reports through email or external applications
  • Integrate reporting with enterprise workflows

 List of APIs

###
GET {{k8}}/taas/api/reports/is-webi-supported
Content-Type: application/json
Authorization: Bearer {{accessToken}}

###
GET {{k8}}/taas/api/reports/period-report-folders
Content-Type: application/json
Authorization: Bearer {{accessToken}}

###
GET {{k8}}/taas/api/reports/ods-reports-config
Content-Type: application/json
Authorization: Bearer {{accessToken}}

###
GET {{k8}}/taas/api/reports/report-template-names
Content-Type: application/json
Authorization: Bearer {{accessToken}}

###
GET {{k8}}/taas/api/reports/report-template-names-by-type-and-recipient
Content-Type: application/json
Authorization: Bearer {{accessToken}}

###
GET {{k8}}/taas/api/reports/report-shortcuts
Content-Type: application/json
Authorization: Bearer {{accessToken}}

###
GET {{k8}}/taas/api/reports/period-report-folders
Content-Type: application/json
Authorization: Bearer {{accessToken}}

###
GET {{k8}}/taas/api/reports/is-webi-supported
Content-Type: application/json
Authorization: Bearer {{accessToken}}

 

API Documentation
https://<tenant-id>.app.commissions.cloud.sap/taas/swagger-ui/index.html#/ 

Bonus Tip

All the tables related to BO are in the TCMP Schema with reporting table starting with TAAS_%

entry.title: Re: How to set up free of charge purchase item in public...
entry.content:
Good day @Ian2345 

Thank you for your question

For a material that must always be purchased free of charge, a purchasing info record with price 0 is not the preferred solution. Condition type PPR0 represents a purchasing price, and the system validates that its rate is greater than zero. A free of charge PO item is instead controlled by the purchasing item indicators, particularly whether an invoice is expected.

The BAdI Change of Purchase Requisition Item (MM_PUR_S4_PR_MODIFY_ITEM) is released for changing selected standard and extension fields in purchase requisition. InvoiceisExpected is part of the purcahase requisition item interface.

See the following reference for more insights.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0e602d466b99490187fcbb30d1dc897c/720dcec59d264114bc4ec19759aa4514.html?locale=en-US

Your code should use a Boolean value:

IF purchaserequisitionitem-material = `XXX`

Purchaserequisitionitemchange-invoiceisexpected = abap false.

ENDIF.

However, this only controls the Invoice Receipt expected indicator in the purchase requisition. It does not provide a material master settling that permanently classifies every future purchase order for that material as free of charge. Depending on how your PO is created, the PO item may still require the purchaser to select Free of Charge manually.

Here is a preferred solution:

You can use the PR BAdI to default InvoiceIsExpected = abap false, then enforce the requirement at purchase order level with the released BAdI Final Check before Saving Purchase Order (BD_MMPUR_FINAL_CHECK_PO). BAdI for custom checks on selected standard purchase order fields are recorded before saving.

See the following reference for more insights.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0e602d466b99490187fcbb30d1dc897c/044700714fc54863b7fa9ea0b5654ba0.html?locale=en-US

Your check should raise an error when the material equals XXX or your PO item is not marked free of charge, then invoice receipt is still expected. This prevents your users or automatic PO creation processes from saving an incorrectly price item. The PO check BAdI validates the document but does not automatically change the standard free of charge field.

See the following reference for more insights.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0e602d466b99490187fcbb30d1dc897c/8676e556cca85267e10000000a441470.html?locale=en-US

Here is a quick Test:

  1. You can publish the PR BAdl implementation in Custom Logic.
  2. Ensure you create a new PR for material XXX
  3. Verify that Invoice Receipt is not expected.
  4. Ensure you convert the PR into PO
  5. Open the item in Manage Purchase Orders or Create Purchase Order – Advanced.
  6. Ensure you confirm that Free of Charge is selected, and no invoice receipt is expected.
  7. Attempt to save after removing the indicator. The final check should block saving.

 

 

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: ACL for Products in SAP Integration Suite Developer Hub
entry.content:
Hi @btpnerd ,

This is the way how can you configure it from scratch:

  • Login to the desired subaccount
  • Create role based on the template called 'Application Developer' -> Click on 'Create a Role'
    • Name it with the desired naming convention
    • Use 'Static' as the 'Source'
    • To fill the 'Values' filed is optional but you can fill with a description/context of the custom role
    • In the Next Page you can select a role collection this is also optional if it is not required you can simply click on 'Next' and 'Finish' the custom role creation
  • Go to 'Role Collection' and click 'Create' and name it with the desired naming convention
  • Now go to the recently created Role Collection and add the newly created Custom Role by clicking the 'Edit' button and than save this (You can have multiple roles assigned to a single role collection)
  • As a last step you can assign to the desired users this custom role

Now you can go to the API Portal configure that end of the scenario:

  • Go to the selected API Product
  • Go to the Permission tab
  • Use the previously created custom role from the drop-down list for the Discovery and/or Subscription to limit the access of this API Product
  • Once you configured and saved it you can go to the Developer Hub to verify this setting

Please let me know if you have any further question about it.

Thank you,
David

entry.title: Re: How to set up free of charge purchase item in public...
entry.content:
Dear @Ian2345,

A purchasing info record with a price of 0 is not the correct solution. Public Cloud a purchase order item for which an invoice is expected cannot have a net price of 0. This explains the error raised for condition type PPR0. In the Manage Purchase Orders app a free-of-charge item is represented by deselecting Invoice Receipt under Process Flow. Once the indicator is deselected the system sets the item price to 0

The attempted BAdI implementation at purchase requisition level is not a viable mechanism for this requirement. As observed, the indicator is not set in the purchase requisition, and even if it were, this would not guarantee a free-of-charge item in the resulting purchase order. The requirement must therefore be enforced at purchase order item level

Automatic defaulting of the Invoice Receipt indicator is not documented as a supported change in the released MM_PUR_S4_PO_MODIFY_ITEM BAdI. Although InvoiceIsExpected is available in the BAdI data model, this does not mean that it is available as a changing field. SAP documents only a defined selection of standard fields that can be modified using this BAdI, and InvoiceIsExpected is not included in that list

The recommended supported solution is therefore:

  1. The purchaser deselects Invoice Receipt for material XXX in the purchase order item. The system then sets the item price to 0
  2. Implement the BAdI Final Check Before Saving Purchase Order (BD_MMPUR_FINAL_CHECK_PO) to prevent the purchase order from being saved when material XXX still has:
    • InvoiceIsExpected = X, or
    • a net price different from 0.

This validation ensures that the material cannot accidentally be ordered as a chargeable item. SAP documents BD_MMPUR_FINAL_CHECK_PO as the released BAdI for implementing customer-specific checks and error messages before a purchase order is saved

The field-control BAdI MM_PUR_S4_PO_FLDCNTRL_SIMPLE is intended only to change the UI properties of supported purchase order item fields, for example to hidden, read-only, mandatory, or optional. It does not assign or change business values. Therefore, it cannot be used to automatically deselect Invoice Receipt and does not replace the final validation implemented with BD_MMPUR_FINAL_CHECK_PO

If completely automatic creation without any manual purchaser action is mandatory, a separate integration-based process would be required to create the purchase order with InvoiceIsExpected = false through a released purchase order interface. Automatic material dependent defaulting of this indicator in the standard Manage Purchase Orders UI is not available through the documented key-user extensibility

More details below:

SAP KBA 3069390 – How to Set a Purchase Order Item as Free of Charges in Manage Purchase Order App

SAP Help – Manage Purchase Orders

SAP Help – App Extensibility: Manage Purchase Orders

SAP KBA 3268822 – Field Control in Purchase Orders and Purchase Requisitions in S/4HANA Cloud

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Spot Tendering in SAP TM -No contract rate for carrier and base charge mandatory for carrier ranking
entry.content:
Overview

Organizations frequently face transportation procurement challenges when a carrier agreement exists, but contract rates are unavailable for specific transportation lanes. In traditional processes, mandatory freight charges defined in calculation sheets can cause charge calculation errors, preventing efficient carrier selection and delaying shipment execution.

SAP S/4HANA Advanced TM provides an effective way to address this challenge through Spot Tendering. By leveraging spot tendering capabilities, organizations can continue procuring transportation services from approved carriers while maintaining operational continuity and supply chain resilience.

After reading this blog, readers will understand the challenges and solutions in spot tendering when contract rates are missing for carrier having freight agreement and subcontracting based on contract rates existing for carriers.

Business Scenario and Challenge:
Consider the spot tender in business scenario where

  1. Certain charges like base freight charge are mandatory for carrier ranking during subcontracting on freight order in SAP TM.
  2. Carrier Freight agreement exists, contract rates are missing on certain lane.
  3. Finance team wants to still have the visibility and audit of charges breakup on freight order as per the agreement with carrier like fuel surcharge, accessorial charges.
  4. But since the base freight charge is mandatory and is missing on the lane, system gives a calculation error on freight order once the spot tender from carrier is awarded.

Solution: A practical and standard solution is to create a dedicated tendering calculation sheet that retains mandatory charge validation during carrier ranking but relaxes mandatory charge requirements after tender execution. An additional calculation sheet can be assigned to freight agreement line items with preconditions. This approach minimizes implementation effort and avoids custom development.

Through this setup we ensure the mandatory charges check to be performed only during the carrier subcontracting (Carrier ranking). And once the tender process is executed, then during charge calculation system selects a calculation sheet which does not have mandatory charge.

  1. Maintain an additional charge calculation sheet line on freight agreements for all required carriers.

Take as an example, create new calculation sheet say ‘TCCS_FTL_TEND_*, as a copy of the existing calculation sheet TCCS_FTL_*. We maintain the required charge lines as not mandatory charges in TCCS_FTL_TEND_* where as the same charge lines are mandatory in TCCS_FTL_*

Sample tendering result from SAP S/4 Test system.

Spot tender sent to carrier, accepted by carrier and awarded to carrier

Charges are calculated upon tender being awarded to carrier.

Conclusion

By combining SAP S/4HANA Advanced TM spot tendering with charge calculation rules and strategies, we can overcome the challenges in carrier ranking during subcontracting, charge calculation in case of spot tendering and provide compliance, efficiency, and financial reporting.

Benefits:
• Improved carrier participation in spot bidding while retaining requirements for contracted carriers
• Finance would have visibility of charges breakup
• Lower operational effort through automation.

entry.title: Configure Standalone Selling Price Using Pricing Conditions in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
Introduction 

Under IFRS 15 and ASC 606, every performance obligation in a multi-element arrangement must be allocated a portion of the transaction price based on its Standalone Selling Price (SSP). But how does SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud actually know what the SSP is for a given product or service? 

The answer lies in a set of pre-configured pricing condition types that the system uses to automatically pass the SSP into Universal Revenue Recognition (URR). In this post, we walk through how these condition types work, where to configure them, and how they appear in the revenue contract — illustrated with a concrete example using a solution order. 

In SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud, a Solution Order can contain multiple business objects — each representing a different line of business — and the way the SSP is defined depends on which business object is involved.  

Sales Item / Service Contract 

The SSP is defined via the standard Pricing Procedure of the Solution Order. SSP condition types (PSAM, PSA1, PSA2) are included in that pricing procedure, and the SSP value is maintained via Manage Prices using condition records in the product master. The calculated value flows automatically to URR. 

Subscription Billing 

Subscription Billing does not use a pricing procedure. Instead, the SSP condition type is defined as an output condition in the Pricing Element Specification. The total amount of PSAM/PSA1/PSA2 is then passed to URR as the SSP of the performance obligation (POB). The detailed configuration for Subscription Billing is covered in a separate blog post — see [link to be added]. 

Customer Project 

For professional services delivered via a Customer Project, there is no pricing procedure and no condition record in the product master. The SSP is entered manually directly in the project when assigning resources. That manually entered value is then converted into the PSAS condition type in the relevant sales order, from where it flows to URR as the SSP of the POB. 

Regardless of which business object is used, URR always receives the total amount of PSAM or PSAS as the SSP value of the performance obligation. The source mechanism differs, but the outcome is consistent. 

The Four Pre-Configured SSP Condition Types 

SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud ships with four standard pricing condition types specifically designated as SSP carriers. You can find them in the configuration activity: 

Manage Pricing → Set Condition Types for Pricing in Sales  

Condition Type 

Description 

Typical Use Case 

PSAM 

Quantity-based SSP 

Standard sales orders, product-based items 

PSAS 

Manually entered SSP 

Professional services (no product master price) 

PSA1 

Monthly-based SSP 

Service contracts — monthly recurring charges 

PSA2 

Daily-based SSP 

Service contracts — daily recurring charges 

Each of these condition types signals to URR that this value represents the standalone selling price of the performance obligation. 

PSAM, PSI1, and PSI2 are automatically included in the standard pricing procedure for solution orders (e.g. A10024) since SAP Public Cloud 2608. They are added at the end of the pricing procedure and — crucially — are marked as Statistical. This means they do not affect the net value of the document; they exist solely to communicate the SSP to revenue accounting. 

Requirement = 2 means, that only when the product has the pricing information maintained, then the pricing information will be displayed. 

Note: If you copy the standard pricing procedure to create your own, make sure you also mark these condition types as Statistical in your custom procedure. 

Note: If you use subscription billing, you can use any of the condition types above.  

Where to Maintain the SSP Value 

Product-Based conditions (PSAM / PSI1 / PSI2) 

For products sold via sales orders or service contracts, the SSP is maintained in the product master: 

Product Master → Pricing Management → Set Material Price for Sales 

Here you define the SSP per condition type. For example: 

  • PSAM = €20.80 per piece (quantity-based, for a standard product) 

  • PSI1 = €50 per month (monthly-based, for a service contract item) 

 

Professional Services (PSAS) 

For professional services, there is no access sequence — meaning the SSP is not derived from the product master. Instead, it is entered manually when you assign resources to the project and capture the demand. The person entering the resource assignment inputs the SSP directly (e.g. €6,000 for a consulting engagement). That value is then converted into the PSAS condition type in the relevant sales order. 

 

A Walk-Through Example: Solution Order 

Let's bring this to life with a solution order containing three business objects: a sales order item, a service contract, and a customer project for professional services. 

Step 1 — Create the Solution Order 

A solution order is created with: 

  • A sales order item (e.g. Operio) with quantity 1 
  • A service contract item with 1 unit over 12 months 
  • A project-based professional services item (Customer Project) 

 

Step 2 — Review Pricing Details 

Opening the pricing details of the solution order, you can see: 

  • For the sales order item: PSAM is picked up from the product master at €20.80 per piece. With a quantity of 5, the SSP value passed to URR is €104.00 (20.80 × 5) 

 

  • For the service contract: PSI1 is picked up at €50 per month. Over 12 months, the SSP value is €600 (50 × 12). PSAM does not appear here because Requirement 2 ensures only the relevant condition type is shown per item type. 

  • For the customer project: PSAS is entered manually as €5,000 when the resource is assigned. It is then converted into the PSAS condition type in the sales order. 

Step 3 — Revenue Contract 

Once the solution order is released, a revenue contract is created automatically. As you can see below the SSP is based on the condition type value in the app ‘Monitor Revenue Contract’ 

  

In the Manage Revenue Accounting Items app, you will see the SSP values transferred per condition  

Business Object 

Condition Type 

SSP Value 

Sales order item 

PSAM 

€10,400 

Service contract 

PSI1 

€600 

Customer project (PS) 

PSAS 

€6,000 

 

Revenue accounting always receives the complete SSP value per item, regardless of how that value was computed (quantity-based, time-based, or manual). The allocation of the transaction price across performance obligations is then performed based on these relative SSP values. 

Key Takeaways 

  • Four condition types (PSAM, PSAS, PSI1, PSI2) are pre-configured in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud to carry the SSP into URR. 
  • These condition types must be marked as Statistical in the pricing procedure — they do not affect the document net value. 
  • SSP values are maintained in the product master for sales order and service contract items, and entered manually for professional services. 
  • SSP is passed through the URR with the condition types above carrying the amount.

entry.title: RF Device EWM SAP - S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
Hi SAP Team,

We are implementing embedded EWM in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud and need to enable RF Device/RF Framework functionality for warehouse operations.

Kindly clarify the following:

  1. Is activation of scope item 63V (RF Framework for S/4HANA Cloud Supply Chain) PERSON_NAME to use RF Device transactions in Public Cloud EWM?
  2. Is there any additional licensing requirement/cost associated with activating this scope item?
  3. Are there any prerequisites or dependent scope items required before activation?
  4. Any known restrictions or limitations of RF Framework in Public Cloud compared to Private Cloud/On-Premise.

Request you to confirm at the earliest as this is blocking our RF configuration for the ongoing implementation.

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: RF Device EWM SAP - S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
Dear SAP Community,

Regarding your questions about using RF devices in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition:

1. Is scope item 63V mandatory to use RF devices?

Yes. If you intend to use the standard RF (Radio Frequency) framework in Embedded EWM, the corresponding scope item 63V – Mobile RF Devices in Warehousing must be activated in your implementation project. This scope item delivers the standard RF functionality for warehouse operations in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

2. Is there any additional licensing required?

No. There is no separate license required specifically for scope item 63V. The RF functionality is part of the standard Embedded EWM capabilities available in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

3. Are there any prerequisites?

The following prerequisites should be fulfilled:

  • Embedded EWM must be implemented.
  • Scope item 63V must be activated in SAP Central Business Configuration (CBC).
  • Warehouse configuration (warehouse number, storage types, bins, etc.) must be completed.
  • Appropriate business roles and RF user settings must be maintained.
  • Presentation devices and RF user settings should be configured using the corresponding Fiori apps.

4. Are there any restrictions in Public Cloud?

The RF framework in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition uses SAP GUI for HTML for productive execution. Unlike Private Cloud or On-Premise, Public Edition does not support customer modifications of the RF framework or custom RF menu development. Only standard RF functionality and limited UI adaptations (for example, via Screen Personas/Adapt UI) are supported. Additionally, there is no need to configure ITS Mobile or communication arrangements, as RF runs natively through SAP GUI for HTML.

Helpful SAP references

The following SAP Notes/KBAs provide additional information:

  • KBA 3671646 - FAQ: Usage of RF (Radio Frequency) Devices in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition - SAP for Me  – FAQ: Usage of RF (Radio Frequency) Devices in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition (overview of RF functionality, supported devices, productive usage, Test RF Environment, restrictions, and best practices).
  • KBA 3672406 - Resource Management for RF device in Fiori Apps for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition - SAP for Me  – Resource Management for RF Device in Fiori Apps for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition (configuration of presentation devices and user settings).
  • SAP Note 3048632 - Information and restrictions for mobile data terminals in Warehouse Management in S/4HANA Cloud or EWM in SAP S/4HANA On-Premise - SAP for Me – Information and restrictions for mobile data terminals in Warehouse Management in SAP S/4HANA Cloud (referenced by KBA 3671646).
  • SAP Note 3340441 - Recommendation for presentation devices and browsers when using RF with SAP GUI for HTML - SAP for Me  – Recommendation for presentation devices and browsers when using RF with SAP GUI for HTML (referenced by KBA 3671646).
  • SAP Note 3364010 - FAQ: EWM RF transactions using SAP GUI for HTML - SAP for Me  – FAQ: EWM RF transactions using SAP GUI for HTML (referenced by KBA 3671646).

Relevant SAP Community discussions

The following SAP Community Q&A may also be helpful:

  • Do we need to use BTP with scope item 63V in order to use RF devices?
    This confirms that SAP BTP is not required for the standard RF functionality in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and recommends following the setup guide for scope item 63V.
  • How to use RF scanner in the EWM S/4HANA Cloud system?
    This explains how productive RF devices access the RF framework using SAP GUI for HTML, provides the productive URL pattern, and recommends the relevant SAP Notes and KBAs for device compatibility and setup.

Hope this helps, please let us know if you have any other concern!

Best regards,

SAP Support

entry.title: Re: Questions regarding BC Upgrade procedures (Manual Tra...
entry.content:
Dear @Harukiy,

Question 1

No, the What’s New Viewer and RASD alone are not sufficient and your practice of reviewing the PDF attachments to SAP Note 3646210 is appropriate. RASD uses the same Preliminary What’s New and final What’s New information as the What’s New Viewer, filtered according to the activated and used scope in your system. These sources cover documented new, changed and deleted functionality but they are not an exhaustive list of every application correction delivered in an individual Hotfix Collection. The HFC PDFs are therefore an important complementary source. Since these PDFs provide only short descriptions, targeted regression testing of critical business processes after the relevant maintenance window remains the final validation step

Question 2

HFC12 was released together with feature update 2602.4 on June 11, 2026. The standard software update is deployed by SAP according to the maintenance schedule. Your manual transport settings control the transport of Business Configuration and extensibility content between your systems they do not control SAP deployment of the HFC itself. The standard screen could therefore change independently of a manual BC transport

I checked both the official final What’s New document for 2602.4 and the HFC12 Hotfix Short Description PDF but I could not find an explicit entry describing this particular ATP Check Selection Screen change. Since RASD uses the same What’s New content, I would not expect a separate RASD entry for this specific change. Product Assistance may reflect the current application behavior but it does not by itself establish when a particular UI adjustment was delivered

Based on the published documentation the exact delivery responsible for this change cannot be identified. If confirmation is required, I would open a case with SAP Support and provide the application name or ID the before and after screenshots the system version and the date when the change became visible

More details below:

SAP KBA 1296527 - How to create a support case for SAP Support - SAP for Me

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Re: CKMHELP - Material Ledger Help Desk Overview
entry.content: Coleti, can it be run in non-production environment without SAP Support?

entry.title: Re: Tengo 3 consultas que realizar sobre modulo de Manufa...
entry.content:
Dear @Nestor_Ramirez,

Question 1.

New production order types can be created in SAP CBC using the Define Production Order Types configuration activity. SAP documents the recommended approach as copying an existing production order type and adjusting it to the required process

The order category remains fixed as category 10 Production Order. Plant-specific settings must additionally be maintained in Define Order-Type-Dependent Parameters for Production Orders, where parameters related to master-data selection, planning, execution and cost accounting are defined. Configuration is therefore possible according to the business requirement but only within the configuration options released by SAP for Public Cloud

Qeustion 2.

New standard value keys can be created using the Define Standard Value Key configuration activity

 

A standard value key can contain up to six standard values, for example setup, machine and labor time. The required parameters are maintained in Define Parameters and can then be used in formulas for scheduling, capacity requirements and costing. The new standard value key must subsequently be assigned to the relevant work centers or resources

Question 3.

A production order without a header material can be created using the Create Production Order Without Material (CO07) app

A WBS element can be used as a non-material settlement receiver in the supported Event-Based Production Cost Posting scenario. However a production order without material referenced to a WBS element is not supported in the Engineer-to-Order scenario. If the requirement is production for project stock the Create Production Order with Project (CO10) app must be used. This scenario requires both a material and a WBS element belonging to a project with project stock

More details below:

SAP Learning – Configuring Production Order Types

SAP Learning – Configuring Manufacturing Master Data

SAP Help – Create Production Order Without Material (CO07)

SAP Help – Settlement Rules of Manufacturing Orders

SAP Community – Production Order Settlement to WBS Elements

SAP Help – Create Production Order with Project (CO10)

SAP KBA 3720746 - Error CO831 in 'Production Order Without Material - Create' (CO07) app - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

SAP KBA 3691249 - None-material ETO order is not supported - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

Best Regards,

Dawid
